verses.text: Make no idols, graven image, standing pillar, image of stone in your land to bow down to it: I am the LORD your God. Keep My sabbaths and reverence My sanctuary: I am the LORD. Guidance: Two negatives, two positives. No idols; yes sabbaths. No images; yes reverence. The covenant begins by clearing both the false worship and the empty calendar. Holiness needs both subtraction and addition.

Make no idols, graven image, standing pillar, image of stone in your land to bow down to it: I am the LORD your God. Keep My sabbaths and reverence My sanctuary: I am the LORD. Guidance: Two negatives, two positives. No idols; yes sabbaths. No images; yes reverence. The covenant begins by clearing both the false worship and the empty calendar. Holiness needs both subtraction and addition.

If ye walk in My statutes — rain in season, land yield increase. Eat to the full, dwell safely. Peace in the land. Sword not pass through. Five chase a hundred, a hundred chase ten thousand. Be fruitful; I establish My covenant. I set My tabernacle among you; walk among you. Brought you from Egypt, broke the yoke. Guidance: God walks among His people — the chapter's heart. Every blessing flows from that one fact. He is not far; He keeps no distance. The lifted neck is His gift.

If ye will not hearken nor do these commandments — I will appoint terror, consumption, burning ague that consume the eyes. Ye shall sow seed in vain; enemies shall eat it. I will set My face against you; smitten before enemies; flee when none pursue. Guidance: First-stage discipline: sickness, lost harvests, flight without pursuit. God's hand begins gently — symptoms a wise heart could read. Disobedience is its own kind of fever.

If yet ye will not hearken, I will punish you seven times more for your sins. I will break the pride of your power; make heaven like iron, earth like brass. Strength spent in vain — the land yields not, trees give no fruit. Guidance: Second-stage discipline — seven times more. Heaven shut like iron, earth hard as brass. The pride of your power has to be broken before the rain returns. God treats stubbornness with weather.

If ye walk contrary to Me — I will bring seven times more plagues for your sins. I will send wild beasts that rob you of children, destroy your cattle, make you few; your highways desolate. Guidance: Third stage — wild animals empty the homes and the roads. Children lost, livestock dead, paths abandoned. God allows the natural world itself to push back when His people refuse to listen.

If ye walk contrary to Me — I will bring seven times more plagues for your sins. I will send wild beasts that rob you of children, destroy your cattle, make you few; your highways desolate. Guidance: Third stage — wild animals empty the homes and the roads. Children lost, livestock dead, paths abandoned. God allows the natural world itself to push back when His people refuse to listen.

If ye will not be reformed — I will walk contrary, punish seven times. Bring a sword — the quarrel of My covenant. Gather within cities; send pestilence. Break the staff of bread; ten women bake in one oven, deliver by weight; ye eat and not be satisfied. Guidance: Fourth stage — siege warfare. Bread rationed by weight, cities full of plague, the sword at the wall. The bread that fed Israel becomes the famine that empties it.

If ye yet will not hearken — I will walk contrary in fury, punish seven times. Ye shall eat your sons and daughters. Cities waste, sanctuaries desolate. Scatter among the heathen; the land shall enjoy her sabbaths in exile. Faint hearts; the sound of a shaken leaf shall chase you. Guidance: The worst stage — cannibalism, exile, terror at falling leaves. The land takes its sabbaths whether Israel rests or not. Exile becomes the corrective rest Israel refused to give.

If they confess iniquity, accept punishment, humble their uncircumcised hearts — I will remember My covenant with Jacob, Isaac, Abraham. The land enjoys her sabbaths. In enemy's land — yet I will not cast them away, not break My covenant: I am the LORD their God. I will remember their ancestors' covenant. Guidance: After every curse, a door. Confession and humbled hearts bring God back. He never breaks the covenant — even after Israel has. This is the grace that sent Christ to the exiled.

These are the statutes, judgments, and laws which the LORD made between Himself and the children of Israel in mount Sinai by the hand of Moses. Guidance: The covenant is signed. Blessings and curses, all spelled out. Sinai is the place where Israel said yes — and where every later generation would be measured.
